JM OS

Web Flashing Command Center
Disconnected

Prerequisites

  • Compatible Browser

    Chrome, Edge, or Opera supporting WebUSB API.

  • USB Drivers (Windows)

    Install Google USB Drivers to allow fastboot interface recognition.

  • Battery 50%+

    Ensure your device has enough power to prevent interruption.

  • Backup Everything

    Flashing will erase all data, music, settings, and photos.

Troubleshooting

If your device is not detected, unplug/replug the USB cable, try a different USB port (USB 2.0 preferred), or check Device Manager for the "Android Device" bootloader driver.

Download Drivers
1
Connect
2
Unlock
3
Build
4
Flash
5
Finish

Connect Your Device

Put your phone into Bootloader (Fastboot) mode and connect it to your PC.

How to enter Bootloader mode:

  1. Power off your device completely.
  2. Press and hold Volume Down + Power buttons simultaneously until the fastboot logo appears.
  3. Plug in your USB-C cable.

Unlock Bootloader

Custom ROMs require an unlocked bootloader to modify system partitions.

Warning: Data Wipe

Unlocking will trigger a factory reset. Ignore if already unlocked.

Choose Firmware

Select the latest JM OS official distribution or upload a local ROM package.

Recommended

Features: Custom bootsounds, Vortex kernel, advanced privacy, premium HSL themes.

Useful for developers flashing custom or experimental AOSP builds.

Flashing System Partitions

Installing JM OS. Please do not close this tab or unplug the device.

Waiting to initialize flash...

Installation Complete!

JM OS has been flashed onto your device successfully.

What's Next?

  • Your device is currently rebooting into the initial system setup.
  • The first boot might take up to 3-5 minutes as system resources compile.
  • Join our support forum or Telegram channel for regular builds and custom bootsounds updates.
